pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/696) - Reactivity Alert lists 'Peroxidizable Compound' and states 'Oxidizes readily in air to form unstable peroxides that may explode spontaneously'

CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/1582) - Reactivity Alert lists 'Peroxidizable Compound' and states 'Subject to peroxidation in the air. Peroxides or their products react exothermically with lithium aluminum hydride'
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/8) - Reactivity Profile does not mention peroxide formation; no 'Peroxidizable Compound' alert listed
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/3232) - Reactivity Alert lists 'Peroxidizable Compound'; Air & Water Reactions section states 'Form explosive peroxide in storage'

CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/3874) - Reactivity Profile does not mention peroxide formation; no 'Peroxidizable Compound' alert listed. Only reference to hydrogen peroxide is regarding hazardous mixtures with other substances

Multiple EHS institutional sources (Weill Cornell, MIT, Yale, University of Kentucky) and CAMEO Chemicals - classified as Group B peroxide former (peroxide hazard on concentration). CAMEO has separate entry for Cumene Hydroperoxide (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/478) confirming formation of peroxide compounds from cumene/isopropylbenzene via oxidation. Cumene hydroperoxide alternate name: 'isopropylbenzene hydroperoxide'

CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4654) - Reactivity Profile states reactions with alkyl halides and oxidizing agents but no mention of peroxide formation; classified as 'Hydrocarbons, Aromatic' with no 'Peroxidizable Compound' alert

CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2272 - Acetic Acid, Glacial) - Reactivity Profile details various hazardous reactions but does not mention peroxide formation; no 'Peroxidizable Compound' alert listed

privileged_info_summary: All eight chemical classifications in the task metadata have been independently verified through CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA) official datasheets and authoritative EHS (Environmental Health & Safety) institutional sources. Group A peroxide formers (diethyl ether, tetrahydrofuran, diisopropyl ether) explicitly form unstable peroxides readily in air and are flagged with 'Peroxidizable Compound' alerts in CAMEO. Isopropylbenzene (cumene) is a verified Group B peroxide former that forms peroxides upon concentration, confirmed by the existence of cumene hydroperoxide as a separate CAMEO chemical entry. Non-peroxide-forming chemicals (acetone, methanol, toluene, acetic acid) show no peroxide formation mechanisms in their CAMEO reactivity profiles and lack peroxidizable compound alerts. The data source is real and authoritative, originating from CAMEO Chemicals, a NOAA/EPA joint chemical hazard database.
